name: Hang Son Doong
description: World's largest cave was only discovered in 1991, and now offers tours to the public.

name: Golden Bridge
description: This spectacular bridge supported by two gigantic hands looks like something out of a fantasy universe.

name: Hanoi's Train Street
description: Twice a day a speeding train passes only inches from the homes of this residential neighborhood.

name: Am Phu Cave
description: This Vietnamese hell cave lets visitors take a walk through a devilish underworld full of demons.

name: Fire-Breathing Dragon Bridge
description: This bridge is embedded with a giant steel dragon that breathes fire from its mouth.

name: Thang Long Water Puppet Theatre
description: Home of the unusual art of water puppetry, one of Vietnam's oldest curiosities.

name: Suoi Tien Cultural Theme Park
description: Have fun at the world's first Buddhist waterslide park.

name: Cafe Pho Co
description: Walk through a clothing store, across a courtyard, and up several flights of stairs for delicious coffee with a side of beautiful views.

name: 100 Roofs Cafe
description: This Vietnamese bar doubles as a multistory labyrinth.

name: The Tomb of Nguyen Emperor Khai Dinh
description: An extravagant monument to an extravagantly unpopular Emperor.

name: Anthony Bourdain and Barack Obama's Dinner Table
description: The spot where the two men ate together now stands enclosed in glass.

name: Dong Van Karst Plateau
description: This striking landscape in northern Vietnam contains both geological and archaeological wonders.

name: Cu Chi Tunnels
description: A nightmare for the claustrophobic lies hidden in Southeast Asia.

name: B-52 Wreckage
description: Monument to a brutal, ineffectual US bombing campaign and a sobering reminder of the human cost of the Vietnam War.

name: Da Lat Crazy House
description: A Surreal flight of architectural fancy in Vietnam's Central Highlands.

name: Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum
description: Ho Chi Minh's perfectly preserved body has been on display here since 1975.

name: Po Shanu Cham Towers
description: These crumbling towers are some of the last remnants of a centuries-old Vietnamese culture.

name: Trompe-l'œil Murals of Hanoi
description: Life-sized painted illusions let pedestrians blend in with the street art.

name: The White Sand Dunes of Vietnam
description: A desert in southeast Asia, complete with a beautiful lake covered in lotus flowers.

name: Hỏa Lò Prison
description: The notorious "Hanoi Hilton" where Vietnamese colonial subjects and later American POWs, including John McCain, were imprisoned.

name: Reaching Out Teahouse
description: Located off a bustling strip, a silent teahouse employs workers with hearing- or speech-related disabilities.

name: Vịnh Mốc Tunnel Complex
description: This network of tunnels housed an entire village for years, and even had a hall for screening movies.

name: Hang Sung Sot
description: Ha Long Bay's "Cave of Surprises" houses a popular phallic rock formation.

name: The Venerable Thich Quảng Đức Monument
description: A memorial to the monk who set himself on fire to protest the persecution of Buddhists in Vietnam.

name: War Remnants Museum
description: Artifacts and memories from the Vietnam war, located in a place that experienced it first hand.

name: Saigon Central Post Office
description: This French colonial post office is arguably the grandest in all of Southeast Asia.

name: Bao tang Dong vat (Hanoi Zoological Museum)
description: A French-Colonial era collection of taxidermy and wet specimens, secreted away in the National University of Hanoi.

name: Japanese Covered Bridge
description: One of Vietnam's most iconic landmarks was built by Japanese traders and is now in danger of sinking.

name: Hospital Cave
description: This cavern was used as a hidden hospital and safe house during the Vietnam War.

name: Snake Village
description: This tiny Vietnamese village is known for eating snakes and serving their still-beating hearts in shots of vodka.

name: Nghia An Hoi Quan Pagoda
description: One of the oldest temples in Ho Chi Minh City, dedicated to a deified Chinese military leader.

name: Lady Buddha
description: Vietnam's largest Buddha statue depicts a female sage.

name: Cao Dai Holy See
description: The lavish temple of the Cao Dai religion that includes Buddha, Mohammed, Joan of Arc, and Victor Hugo.

name: Notre-Dame Cathedral Basilica of Saigon
description: Witnesses claim the Virgin Mary statue outside the church once mysteriously shed a single tear.

name: The Fairy Stream
description: This whimsically named Vietnamese stream is closer to a walkway than a waterway.

name: Côn Đảo Prison
description: The wartime prison now features haunting sculpted reenactments of the atrocities that took place there.

name: The World Coffee Museum
description: Drink and learn at this striking ode to coffee culture and history.

name: Clay Tunnel in Dalat
description: A sculpture park that takes the use of clay to a whole new level.

name: Reunification Palace
description: Meant to be a lavish presidential home, the palace became a symbol of the fall of South Vietnam.

name: Gành Đá Dĩa
description: This unique formation is characterized by hexagonal basalt rocks stacked in unique formations.

name: Secret Weapon Bunker
description: Nearly three tons of weapons from the Vietnam War still remain in this small, hidden museum.

name: Lang Tan Temple (Temple of the Whale)
description: Home to what locals claim is the largest whale skeleton in Southeast Asia, which is worshiped yearly.

name: Thien Mu Pagoda
description: Giant pagoda houses the car that drove Thích Quảng Đức to his death by self-immolation.

name: Bún Chả Hàng Quạt
description: Don’t miss the local staple of rice vermicelli with charcoal-grilled pork patties and pork belly.

name: Nam Trân
description: This antique shop offers affordable 15th-century Vietnamese ceramics salvaged from a local shipwreck.

name: An Bang Cemetery
description: A cityscape of burial tombs that you can easily lose yourself in.

name: The Former U.S. Embassy to South Vietnam
description: This historic spot was where America's involvement in the Vietnam War officially came to an end.

name: Phở Hàng Trống
description: For true northern-style phở with broth as clear as consommé, hunt for this restaurant hidden within a residential home.

name: The Tomb of Lê Văn Duyệt
description: An ornate tomb pays tribute to one of the most influential and cherished military leaders in Vietnam's history.

name: Hue Cafe Roastery
description: Order your coffee crowned with salted whipped cream or blended with coconut and ice.

name: The Massive Darahoa Rooster
description: Fowl, love and death in Vietnam.

name: T1 Operation Command Bunker
description: Under Hanoi’s medieval citadel sits a secret military headquarters from the final years of the Vietnam War.

name: Christ of Vung Tau
description: Shoes and revealing shirts are not allowed inside this giant Vietnamese Christ.

name: Bánh Cuốn Bà Xuân
description: This steamed rice crepe specialist has been rolling up its namesake dish for over 30 years.

name: Statue of Lenin
description: A gift from the Soviet Union in the 1980s, this is the only statue of the Soviet dictator in Southeast Asia.

name: Bún Riêu Gánh
description: This crab paste noodle soup specialist has been doling out its namesake dish for more than four decades.

name: Bún Thang 29 Hàng Hành
description: This time-honored eatery near Hoàn Kiếm Lake serves the classic pairing of rice vermicelli soup and savory sticky rice.

name: Cơm Tấm Nguyễn Văn Cừ
description: Vietnam’s everyman “broken rice” dish is given one major upgrade at this humble institution.

name: Ănăn Saigon
description: A refugee returns to his homeland to open Ho Chi Minh City’s first Michelin-starred restaurant.

name: Bà Nà Hills
description: Founded by French colonists in 1919, this mountain resort has been transformed into one-of-a-kind theme park.

name: Phở Xào Bà Thanh Béo
description: Order your phở stir-fried, sans soup.

name: Chợ Lớn Mosque
description: One of Vietnam's only mosques pays tribute to the country's Muslim minority.

name: Nhớ Tuyết
description: For a taste of Vietnamese gastropub culture, look no further than this convivial seafood joint that stays open till midnight.

name: Hổ Quyền (Tiger Arena)
description: Perhaps the only one of its kind, the nearly-forgotten site once held mortal (and rigged) gladiatorial games between tigers and imperial war elephants.

name: My Son Sanctuary
description: Showcasing some of the best-preserved temples of the Champa Kingdom.

name: Tay Nguyen Biological Museum
description: This former monastery in Vietnam now houses a chocolate shop and museum filled with centuries-old taxidermy specimens.

name: Ho Chi Minh Statue
description: Out of all the statues of Ho Chi Minh in Vietnam, this one is the tallest and the most impressive.

name: Sơn Mỹ Memorial
description: A memorial dedicated to the victims of the My Lai Massacre.

name: Da Nang Cathedral
description: This candy-pink cathedral of Da Nang is also known as the Chicken Church.

name: Grave of Võ Thị Sáu
description: Executed at age 19, this Vietnamese schoolgirl is now a national hero and symbol of revolutionary spirit.

name: Museum of Cham Sculpture
description: Home to the world's largest collection of ancient Champa's sculptural works, featuring many Hindu and Buddhist deities.

name: Frog Garden Fountain
description: Unbeknownst to many, this fountain was erected as a tomb and still holds the remains of a Frenchman.

name: Phu Quoc Prison History Museum
description: This museum stands out for its poignant portrayal of the atrocities of the Vietnam War and serves as a vital educational tool for promoting peace and understanding.

name: Museum of Sa Huỳnh Culture
description: A small museum dedicated to Vietnam’s lesser-known Iron Age culture.

name: Bảo Tàng Tarot (Museum of Tarot)
description: A mystical-meets-modern mix of centuries-old divination decks and Pokémon cards ready to predict your destiny.

name: Muc Lien's 18 Gates of Hell
description: Under a breathtaking Buddhist temple in Vietnam is a basement exhibit that takes visitors to hell and back.

name: Omamori Spa West Lake
description: This spa in Hanoi is staffed exclusively by the visually impaired.

name: Dalat Railway Station
description: This 1930s train station still gets hundreds of visitors a day despite the fact it only makes a 7-kilometer journey.

name: Hồ Thuỷ Tiên
description: Vietnam's feral abandoned water park offers access to a post-apocalyptic funscape, now with fewer crocodiles.
